title: polygala sibirica subsp. sibirica
intro: Polygala sibirica subsp. sibirica, also known as Sibirian milkwort or Sibirian polygala, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving respiratory issues and promoting fluid balance. The health benefits of Polygala sibirica subsp. sibirica include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and asthma, as well as support kidney function and improve overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other herbal remedies.
